Paper review about Blunted ventral striatal responses to anticipated reward foreshadow problematic drug use in novelty-seeking adolescents (Buchel et al., 2017)

October 12, 2020

I gave a presentation on the paper “Blunted ventral striatal responses to anticipated reward foreshadow problematic drug use in novelty-seeking adolescents” by Buchel et al. (2017). I believe predicting future drug use with longitudinal data could help identify the mechanism of substance use/misuses such as risk factors, and further lead to effective early interventions. Since I’m working with other large-sample longitudinal data ( ABCD project ), and looking forward to do such prediction, this paper was a great reference. Also, I hosted a class discussion in the seminar in psychopathology, and we mainly talked about “socioeconomic factors that we should consider when investigating substance use” and “effective intervention approach considering possible side effects”.

Date: 12 October, 2020
Event: Seminar in Psychopathology
Location: Seoul, South Korea
Paper: Link
Slides: Download pdf
